title: Short-Form Video and Playable Ads Dominate iGaming Ad Formats in 2026
summary: Short-form video (≤15s) achieves 3.8–6.1% CTR with 180–320% ROI, while interstitial/playable formats deliver 4.5–7.2% CTR with 200–380% ROI. These formats significantly outperform declining popunders (0.2–0.8% CTR) and display banners (0.1–0.4% CTR) in 2026.

title: Playable Ads Achieve 4.8 IPM Average, Outperforming Video by 66% in 2026
summary: Liftoff's 2026 Mobile Ad Creative Report shows playable ads deliver 4.8 IPM on average, surpassing video (2.9 IPM) by 66% and static (1.4 IPM) by 243%. Playable ads saw 23% YoY IPM increase driven by improved HTML5 rendering and Apple SKAdNetwork 5.0 support.

title: Creative Fatigue Accelerates to 9.2 Days for Video Ads on Meta in 2026
summary: Creative fatigue onset for video ads on Meta has accelerated from 14 days in 2024 to 9.2 days in 2026, a 34% acceleration. Recommended refresh cadence is every 7 days. Static images now show fatigue in 6.5 days (35% acceleration from 2024).

title: Top Advertisers Produce 2,400–2,600 Creative Variations Quarterly in 2025
summary: ContentGrip's 2026 Gaming UA report shows top advertisers ($4M+/quarter) produced 2,400–2,600 creative variations per quarter in 2025, up 25–30% YoY. Mid-tier advertisers ($0.5–1M) stagnated unless scaling beyond 1,000 creatives, while smaller advertisers boosted production 20–40% YoY.

title: Multi-Variant Campaigns with AI Testing Reduce CPA by 31% in 2026
summary: Industry analysis shows campaigns with 5+ creative variants and AI-assisted testing reduced CPA by an average of 31% compared to static single-creative campaigns in 2026. Creative is now viewed as a performance lever, not a design afterthought.

title: Playable Ads Achieve 11.4% D7 ROAS in Casual Gaming Vertical (2026)
summary: Liftoff's 2026 benchmarks show playable ads deliver 11.4% D7 ROAS in casual gaming, outperforming video (8.2%) and static (5.1%). In hyper-casual, video leads at 14.1% D7 ROAS, with playable at 18.3% D30 ROAS, demonstrating vertical-specific performance variations.

title: Video Accounts for 71% of Ad Spend Despite Playable Ads' Superior IPM
summary: Sensor Tower's 2026 Q1 Ad Intelligence data shows video remains the highest-volume format, accounting for 71% of total ad spend across top 200 advertisers, despite playable ads delivering superior IPM performance (4.8 vs 2.9 IPM).

title: Popunder Ads Remain High-Performing Workhorse Format for iGaming in 2026
summary: Despite declining CTR (0.2–0.8%), popunders remain the 'workhorse' for iGaming affiliates in 2026, delivering landing pages directly with massive daily volumes and accessible CPM rates. They perform best for volume-first CPM campaigns rather than CPA-focused efforts.

title: Native In-Feed Ads Achieve 1.9–3.4% CTR with 140–260% ROI in 2026
summary: Native in-feed ads deliver 1.9–3.4% CTR with 140–260% ROI in 2026, making them effective for retargeting and content-match audiences. This format shows rising trend status alongside short-form video and playable formats.

title: Playable Ads Show 27% Acceleration in Creative Fatigue Resistance (2024-2026)
summary: Playable ads demonstrate the slowest creative fatigue acceleration at 27% (from 21 days in 2024 to 15.4 days in 2026), compared to video at 34% and static at 35%. Recommended refresh cadence for playables is every 12 days, offering longer campaign longevity.
